title: Jovibarba pseudohirta
taxonomy: Jovibarba pseudohirta is classified in the plant family Crassulaceae, commonly known as the stonecrop family. This family comprises over 1,400 species of succulents and other plants. The Jovibarba genus is a relatively small group, containing only a few species of alpine plants. Jovibarba pseudohirta is native to the high-altitude regions of Europe, where it grows in rocky outcrops and acidic soils. Its classification within the Crassulaceae family reflects its evolutionary relationships with other succulent plants.

intro: Jovibarba pseudohirta, also known as the False Hairy Jovibarba, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the saxifrage family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Jovibarba pseudohirta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
